title: Apollo Justice: Ace Attorney
creator: Shu Takumi
notes: Fourth main entry in the Ace Attorney series. Introduces Apollo Justice as the new protagonist. Cases span a rigged poker game murder, a hit-and-run, a concert shooting, and a seven-year-old cold case.

persons.name: Apollo Justice
persons.notes: Defense attorney, new protagonist of the game

persons.name: Phoenix Wright
persons.notes: Former defense attorney, now a poker player. Mentors Apollo Justice.

persons.name: Kristoph Gavin
persons.notes: Apollo's mentor and senior attorney, revealed to be the overarching villain

persons.name: Shadi Smith
persons.notes: Alias used by Zak Gramarye during the poker game. Shot by Kristoph Gavin.

persons.name: Pal Meraktis
persons.notes: Surgeon killed in Turnabout Corner; had performed illegal surgery on Wocky Kitaki

persons.name: Alita Tiala
persons.notes: Wocky Kitaki's fiancée; killed Pal Meraktis to prevent exposure of bullet still in Wocky

persons.name: Machi Tobaye
persons.notes: Blind Borginian pianist; initially appeared to be the killer of LeTouse but was a pawn

persons.name: Romein LeTouse
persons.notes: Interpol agent investigating Borginian cocoon smuggling; shot during a concert

persons.name: Daryan Crescend
persons.notes: Detective and guitarist; the true killer of Romein LeTouse, was the cocoon smuggler

persons.name: Drew Misham
persons.notes: Forger and artist; poisoned by atroquinine on a stamp licked by him

persons.name: Vera Misham
persons.notes: Drew's daughter; poisoned by atroquinine (attempted murder, survived)

persons.name: Magnifi Gramarye
persons.notes: Legendary magician; shot himself (suicide) after being blackmailed by Kristoph Gavin

persons.name: Zak Gramarye
persons.notes: Magician, Shadi Smith is his alias; fled after acquittal, murdered by Kristoph Gavin

deaths.motive_detail: Kristoph Gavin killed Shadi Smith (Zak Gramarye) after losing a poker game; Zak had chosen Phoenix Wright over Kristoph as his attorney seven years prior
deaths.notes: Victim's true identity is Zak Gramarye, a famous magician in hiding

deaths.motive_detail: Alita killed Meraktis to prevent him from revealing that Wocky Kitaki still had a bullet near his heart, which would expose her plan to inherit the Kitaki fortune
deaths.notes: Meraktis was struck with a flower pot

deaths.motive_detail: Daryan killed Interpol agent LeTouse to conceal his involvement in Borginian cocoon smuggling
deaths.notes: LeTouse was an undercover Interpol agent. Daryan staged the scene to frame Machi Tobaye.
Machi fell from the stage catwalk while trying to escape; accidental death during investigation events

deaths.motive_detail: Magnifi shot himself after leaving instructions to his apprentices to shoot him; he was terminally ill and used his death as one final test
deaths.notes: Magnifi's suicide was staged as a murder scene. Flashback case set seven years before the main game.

deaths.motive_detail: Kristoph poisoned the stamp with atroquinine to silence Drew Misham, who had forged evidence for him seven years earlier
deaths.notes: Drew died by licking a poisoned commemorative stamp sent by Kristoph Gavin

deaths.motive_detail: Kristoph poisoned both the stamp and a bottle of nail polish with atroquinine to eliminate all witnesses to his forgery
deaths.notes: Vera was poisoned via nail polish containing atroquinine; she survived after receiving treatment

title: Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney – Dual Destinies
creator: Takeshi Yamazaki
notes: Fifth main entry. Phoenix Wright returns as a licensed attorney. The game's overarching theme is the 'Dark Age of the Law.' Introduces Athena Cykes as a new co-protagonist.

persons.name: Phoenix Wright
persons.notes: Veteran defense attorney, returned to active practice

persons.name: Apollo Justice
persons.notes: Defense attorney, co-protagonist of the game

persons.name: Athena Cykes
persons.notes: New defense attorney at the Wright Anything Agency, specializes in emotional perception

persons.name: Clay Terran
persons.notes: Apollo's best friend and astronaut; murdered by Ted Tonate in the bombing, and later stabbed by the Phantom

persons.name: Ted Tonate
persons.notes: Bomb disposal expert and detective; murdered Bobby Fulbright and killed Clay Terran with a bomb

persons.name: Bobby Fulbright
persons.notes: Detective impersonated by the Phantom; actually murdered prior to the game's events

persons.name: The Phantom
persons.notes: International spy of unknown identity who impersonated Bobby Fulbright throughout the game

persons.name: Rex Kyubi
persons.notes: Chief of Tenma Town; murdered during the festival

persons.name: Florent L'Belle
persons.notes: Cosmetics mogul; murdered Rex Kyubi for the mining rights to Tenma Town

persons.name: Constance Courte
persons.notes: Professor at Themis Legal Academy; murdered by Aristotle Means

persons.name: Aristotle Means
persons.notes: Professor at Themis Legal Academy; killed Courte to protect his forged evidence scheme

persons.name: Solomon Starbuck
persons.notes: Astronaut falsely accused of the HAT-2 explosion and Clay Terran's death

persons.name: Jack Shipley
persons.notes: Owner of Shipshape Aquarium; murdered in the DLC case Turnabout Reclaimed

persons.name: Marlon Rimes
persons.notes: Orca trainer; killed Jack Shipley to avenge the death of an orca he believed Shipley caused

persons.name: Yuri Cosmos
persons.notes: Director of the Cosmos Space Center; nearly framed for the HAT-2 sabotage

deaths.motive_detail: Ted Tonate set off the HH-3000 bomb to destroy evidence of his theft; Clay Terran was killed in the explosion
deaths.notes: Clay survived the initial blast but was later stabbed by the Phantom; the bombing is also the inciting incident of the game

deaths.motive_detail: The Phantom killed and assumed the identity of detective Bobby Fulbright to infiltrate the investigation
deaths.notes: Fulbright had already been killed before the game's events; his identity was stolen by the Phantom spy

deaths.motive_detail: Florent L'Belle killed Kyubi to gain access to the Hidden Shrine and secure gold mining rights for his company
deaths.notes: Kyubi was killed during the Nine-Tails Vale festival; the killer exploited the legend of the yokai for misdirection

deaths.motive_detail: Professor Means killed Courte because she discovered his scheme to have students forge evidence in mock trials, and threatened to expose him
deaths.notes: Courte was struck with a flagpole on the academy grounds

deaths.motive_detail: The Phantom stabbed Clay Terran aboard the HAT-2 rocket to prevent him from reporting what he had witnessed during the mission
deaths.notes: Clay survived the initial bombing but was stabbed by the Phantom on the rocket; this is the true murder central to the second half of the game

deaths.motive_detail: Marlon Rimes blamed Shipley for the death of Ora Shipley (a killer whale) and killed him in retaliation
deaths.notes: DLC case. Shipley was drowned in the aquarium pool; an orca named Orla was initially suspected.

title: Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney – Spirit of Justice
creator: Takeshi Yamazaki
notes: Sixth main entry. Cases span two settings: the Kingdom of Khura'in (where defense attorneys are punished with the accused) and Los Angeles. Introduces the Divination Seance mechanic.

persons.name: Phoenix Wright
persons.notes: Defense attorney operating in the Kingdom of Khura'in

persons.name: Apollo Justice
persons.notes: Defense attorney, holds down the Wright Anything Agency in LA while Phoenix is away

persons.name: Athena Cykes
persons.notes: Defense attorney at the Wright Anything Agency

persons.name: Rayfa Padma Khura'in
persons.notes: Crown Princess of Khura'in; performs the Divination Seance. Later revealed to be Dhurke's adopted daughter.

persons.name: Tahrust Inmee
persons.notes: High priest of Khura'in; found dead in the Inner Sanctum. His death is actually a suicide designed to protect his wife.

persons.name: Rheel Neh'mu
persons.notes: Rebel fighter found dead near the Inner Sanctum; killed by Tahrust Inmee

persons.name: Nayna Inmee
persons.notes: Tahrust's wife; member of the Defiant Dragons rebel group. Tahrust killed to protect her.

persons.name: Datz Are'bal
persons.notes: Leader of the Defiant Dragons rebel movement; falsely accused in Case 2

persons.name: Pees'lubn Andistan'dhin
persons.notes: Monk and musician at the Inner Sanctum; the true killer of Rheel Neh'mu

persons.name: Roger Retinz
persons.notes: TV producer, former Amazing Nine-Tails magician; orchestrated Manov Mistree's death to frame Trucy

persons.name: Manov Mistree
persons.notes: Magician performing with Trucy; killed by Roger Retinz's trap during a live show

persons.name: Trucy Wright
persons.notes: Magician and Phoenix's adopted daughter; falsely accused of Manov Mistree's death

persons.name: Archie Buff
persons.notes: Archaeologist and folklorist; murdered by Bonny de Famme in case 4

persons.name: Bonny de Famme
persons.notes: Magician's assistant; killed Archie Buff with the help of her twin sister Betty

persons.name: Betty de Famme
persons.notes: Bonny's twin sister; conspired with Bonny to murder Archie Buff

persons.name: Dhurke Sahdmadhi
persons.notes: Leader of the Defiant Dragons and Apollo's biological father; murdered by Inga Karkhuul Khura'in

persons.name: Inga Karkhuul Khura'in
persons.notes: Minister of Justice of Khura'in and the Queen's consort; murdered Dhurke and later killed by Ga'ran

persons.name: Ga'ran Sigatar Khura'in
persons.notes: Queen of Khura'in; the ultimate villain who killed Inga and engineered the entire conspiracy

persons.name: Amara Sigatar Khura'in
persons.notes: Former queen of Khura'in and Rayfa's biological mother; survived an attempted murder by Ga'ran years earlier

persons.name: Paul Atishon
persons.notes: Politician and candidate; involved in the murder of Archie Buff and theft of the Founder's Orb

persons.name: Zeh'lot
persons.notes: Rebel/bystander killed by Pees'lubn Andistan'dhin in Case 2

deaths.motive_detail: Andistan'dhin killed Neh'mu to silence him and protect his own secrets
deaths.notes: Initially Tahrust Inmee's death is the central mystery; Neh'mu's death is uncovered as part of the investigation. Andistan'dhin is the true killer.

deaths.motive_detail: Tahrust took his own life to create a false murder scene that would exonerate his wife Nayna from rebel activities
deaths.notes: Tahrust's death appears to be a murder in the Inner Sanctum; the Divination Seance is used to investigate. The twist is that he died by suicide to protect Nayna.

deaths.motive_detail: Andistan'dhin killed Zeh'lot as part of his effort to suppress rebel activity and protect himself
deaths.notes: Secondary death uncovered during the investigation of Case 2

deaths.motive_detail: Retinz rigged a magic trick prop to cause Mistree to fall fatally; he framed Trucy Wright as revenge for the Gramarye family expelling him
deaths.notes: Mistree fell through a trapdoor rigged to drop him fatally. Retinz's goal was to frame and destroy Trucy Wright.

deaths.motive_detail: Bonny and Betty de Famme killed Buff to steal the Founder's Orb at the behest of Paul Atishon
deaths.notes: Buff was killed with a bust. The case is entangled with the political conspiracy around Atishon and the Founder's Orb.

deaths.motive_detail: Inga shot Dhurke to crush the Defiant Dragons rebellion and preserve the power structure he and Ga'ran had built
deaths.notes: Dhurke's death is a major twist; he had been operating as a ghost-like presence via the Divination Seance despite already being dead.

deaths.motive_detail: Ga'ran poisoned Inga once he became a liability; Inga had leverage over her but she struck first to protect her secrets
deaths.notes: Inga's death reveals that Ga'ran is the true villain behind the entire game's conspiracy

deaths.motive_detail: Ga'ran attempted to kill Queen Amara years before the game's events to seize the throne of Khura'in
deaths.notes: Amara survived the assassination attempt and was kept hidden. Revealed as a flashback twist in the finale.
